data.title: Anywhere Real Estate reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Anywhere Real Estate press release (NYSE:HOUS): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.58 misses by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $1.36B (+8.8% Y/Y) beats by $80M.
  • Operating EBITDA of $52 million, up $24 million year-over-year.
  • Combined closed transaction volume increased 13% year-over-year with units up about 3% and price up 9%.

  • 2025 Financial Estimates

    The Company expects to realize further cost savings of approximately $100 million in 2025, which we expect will be offset in part by inflationary pressures and investments as we look to make significant progress transforming our business.

    The Company expects Operating EBITDA for full year 2025 to be about $350 million. The largest variable in this estimate is the performance of the housing market. The Company expects normal seasonal volumes throughout the year.

    The Company expects its Free Cash Flow excluding one-time items to be similar to 2024. Free cash flow, like Operating EBITDA, is driven by the overall housing market and may be impacted by additional investments we make to drive growth and advance our technology strategy.

data.title: Knife River GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.07, revenue of $657.2M beats by $4.9M
data.description:
• Knife River press release (NYSE:KNF): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.07.

data.title: Navios Maritime Non-GAAP EPS of $2.61 beats by $0.11, revenue of $332.52M beats by $41.69M

• Navios Maritime press release (NYSE:NMM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.61 beats by $0.11.

data.title: Steve Madden acquires luxury UK footwear brand Kurt Geiger
data.description:
Steve Madden (NASDAQ:SHOO) announced on Thursday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire UK-based Kurt Geiger from a group led by international private equity firm Cinven for an enterprise value of approximately £289 million in cash.

Steve Madden (NASDAQ:SHOO) intends to fund the transaction with a combination of committed debt financing and cash on hand. The transaction is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2025, subject to the receipt of required regulatory clearance and other customary closing conditions. The board has unanimously approved the transaction.

Kurt Geiger was founded in 1963, growing from a single store on London's Bond Street to an international fashion powerhouse. The London-based company operates around 70 stores worldwide and has multiple concessions within department stores, including prestigious locations like Harrods and Selfridges.

In addition to Kurt Geiger London, Kurt Geiger’s brand portfolio includes KG Kurt Geiger and Carvela. Kurt Geiger also operates footwear concessions within luxury and premium department stores in the United Kingdom, including Harrods and Selfridges, where it sells both its own and third-party brands. For the twelve months ended February 1, 2025, Kurt Geiger had estimated revenue of approximately £400 million.

"Kurt Geiger London’s unique brand image, high-quality and statement-making styles and compelling value proposition have driven success across multiple product categories, led by handbags. Its differentiated and elevated positioning within the market – and its alignment with our strategic initiatives of expanding in international markets, accessories categories and direct-to-consumer channels – make this a highly attractive and complementary addition to our portfolio," highlighted Steve Madden CEO Edward Rosenfeld.

data.title: Calian Group Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.88, revenue of C$185M; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Calian Group press release (OTCPK:CLNFF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.88.
  • Revenue of C$185M (+3.2% Y/Y). Organic growth was down 5%
  • Gross margin at 31.8%, slightly down from 32.5% last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of C$18 million, down from C$21 million last year.
  • Operating free cash flow of C$13 million, down from C$17 million last year.
  • Net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio of 0.6x.
  • Repurchased 101,350 shares in consideration of C$4.9 million.
  • Announced new U.S. subsidiary to focus on U.S. government and defence

  • Guidance Reiterated

    The table below presents the FY25 guidance based on the new definition of adjusted EBITDA.

    Guidance for the year ended September 30, 2025 FY24 Results YOY Growth at Midpoint

    Revenue 800,000 840,000 880,000 746,611 12 %
    Adj. EBITDA1 96,000 101,000 106,000 92,159 10 %

data.title: Elliott builds nearly $5B stake in BP - FT
data.description:
Elliott Management has disclosed a nearly 5% stake in BP (NYSE:BP) worth almost £3.8B (~$4.75B), making it the company's third largest shareholder, Financial Times reported Thursday.

The activist investor is trying to push the troubled company to cut spending on renewable energy and make big divestments, the report said.

Only BlackRock and Vanguard hold larger stakes in BP (NYSE:BP), at 9% and 5% respectively.

News of an Elliott stake broke last weekend, and CEO Murray Auchincloss pledged a "fundamental reset" of BP's (BP) strategy as he released another set of disappointing quarterly results on Tuesday.

BP (BP) is due to unveil its plans at a capital markets day on February 26, and it is not yet clear if those plans will be sufficient to satisfy Elliott, which hopes to see an aggressive chair supported by an engaged board pushing for the reset, according to the report.

data.title: Nissan, Honda, and Mitsubishi officially ended their merger and integration talks
data.description:
Nissan Motor Co. (OTCPK:NSANY) and Honda Motor (NYSE:HMC) have called off talks on a deal that would have created the world's third-largest carmaker. The development was widely anticipated after discussions had stalled in recent weeks to break off any merger momentum.

Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY), Honda (NYSE:HMC), and Mitsubishi Motors (OTCPK:MMTOF) formally agreed to terminate a memorandum of understanding.

"Going forward, Nissan and Honda will collaborate within the framework of a strategic partnership aimed at the era of intelligence and electrified vehicles, striving to create new value and maximize the corporate value of both companies," read a statement.

Following the merger announcement, Bloomberg reported that KKR & Co. (KKR) is weighing an investment in Nissan Motor (OTCPK:NSANY) Sources indicated that the private equity firm is in the early stages of evaluating an equity or debt investment to improve Nissan's (OTCPK:NSANY) financial position.

Shares of Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) edged 0.2% higher in premarket trading, while Honda Motor (HMC) gained 1.6%. Toyota Motor (TM) was up 0.7% in the early session.

data.title: Cellebrite beats top-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Cellebrite press release (NASDAQ:CLBT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 in-line.
  • Revenue of $109M (+17.2% Y/Y) beats by $1.86M.

  • ARR grew 25% to $395.9 million.

  • Fourth-quarter 2024 adjusted EBITDA of $28.8 million, 26.4% adjusted EBITDA margin.

  • Subscription revenue was $95.1 million, up 21% year-over-year.

  • Annual Recurring Revenue of $395.9 million, up 25% year-over-year.

  • Recurring revenue dollar-based net retention rate of 124%.

  • The Company’s first-quarter and full-year expectations for 2025 are as follows:

    First-Quarter 2025 Expectations(as of 2/13/25) Full-Year 2025 Expectations(as of 2/13/25)
    ARR $406 million - $411 million $480 million - $495 million
    Annual growth 22% - 24% 21% - 25%
    Revenue $107 million - $112 million vs. $105.54M consensus $480 million - $490 million vs. $465.57M consensus
    Annual growth 19% - 25% 20% - 22%
    Adjusted EBITDA $22 million - $24 million $113 million - $123 million
    Adjusted EBITDA margin ~21% 24% - 25%
